sentence_reviews.text: Monte Carlo tree search alternates selection, expansion, simulation, and backup to allocate search effort toward promising actions under uncertainty.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA Monte Carlo tree search/game-search material.
sentence_reviews.notes: The phases and purpose match the standard MCTS description.

sentence_reviews.text: Search and self-evaluation can multiply cost while preserving confident errors when states, tools, or verifiers are weak. Its direct dependencies include Graph search, BFS/DFS & A*, Probability theory.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Synthesized from cited node sources plus the atlas dependency context in data/atlas.json.
sentence_reviews.notes: Limitation is reviewed as a broad synthesis/tradeoff statement for the family-level node, not as a direct quotation from one source.

sentence_reviews.text: Monte Carlo tree search explores alternatives using the state, scoring, or search process in Graph search, BFS/DFS & A*.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA tree/graph search and MCTS material.
sentence_reviews.notes: MCTS is a tree-search method, but the dependency label includes BFS/DFS/A*, which are not required by MCTS.

sentence_reviews.text: Monte Carlo tree search explores alternatives using the state, scoring, or search process in Probability theory.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA Monte Carlo simulation/search under uncertainty material.
sentence_reviews.notes: Monte Carlo rollouts and value estimates directly use probabilistic sampling/statistical estimation.

sentence_reviews.text: Monte Carlo tree search explores alternatives using the state, scoring, or search process in Game theory & decision theory.
sentence_reviews.notes: MCTS is strongly supported for game search; decision-theory framing is synthesized.

sentence_reviews.text: In this atlas graph, Monte Carlo tree search is modeled as depending on Graph search, BFS/DFS & A*, Probability theory and feeding downstream nodes such as World-model rollout & imagination, Game-playing, control & simulation agents.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: data/atlas.json canonical node and dependency graph; generated from node dependencies and downstream consumers.
sentence_reviews.notes: This is an explicit internal atlas-graph positioning claim, not an external-world necessity claim.

edge_reviews.notes: Tree/graph search is central, but BFS/DFS/A* are neighboring search methods rather than direct ingredients.
Monte Carlo simulation and backup estimates directly support the relationship and core strength.
Game-search evidence supports the common dependency; decision-theory breadth is synthesized.
